14026756487.description:
As a byproduct of the buffer size changes required to meet Nimbus latency, it is now required for IOCA to use all 14-bits of RTID and 13-bits of UQID.


While internally IPs use all 13-bits of UQID, the CXL spec only defines 12, which requires either the fabric or the agent to perform re-mapping.


SCA UQID usage:



The IP has already been working on this change, but the SoC team wanted a separate feature to be filed to track the integration/validation effort.


https://docs.intel.com/documents/iparch/cxlcm/HAS/Gen5/features/IPCXL-402/IPCXL-402_FHAS.html
14026756487.title: [NWP] CXL to allow more than 4K UQID from fabric
14026756487.subject: feature

14026603527.description:
NWP topology changes has a few gaps for RAS: 

1.) SMI sources: we need to define a full list of SMI sources for NWP. SMI messages need to be reported to the root RAS IP, which supports up to 64 sources. 

The table below highlights the new sources for NWP in green: 


2.) Each pcie error source needs a unique bitmap ID. The bit map ID is a 5 bit field, NWP has more than 32 agents reporting to the root ras IP, so we need more than 5 bits. [The memory controller is a big part of this, since we have 32 instances of the memory controller]

attached slides have details for solution

To avoid making the IPs change one way to fix this would be to have half the memory controllers report pcie errors to one root-1 RAS IP, and the other half report to another root-1 RAS IP. We have free space in the UIO RAS IPs.

The root-1 ras IP can’t receive SMI messages, so the memory controller would still be strapped to send these to the Root RAS IP. Need to create full list of PCIE error sources reporting to each RAS IP and confirm RAS IP changes aren't needed.
14026603527.title: [NWP] RAS changes for NWP topology changes
14026603527.subject: feature

14025926200.description:
The instructions CLFLUSH, CLFLUSHOPT, CLWB should Invalidate every level of cache that contains the referenced linear address

The PWB requires additional support from Cbo 

Nominally, and writeback completes in PWB; however, a flush instr must cause the PWB entry, if it exists, to be evicted.


From talking to ARCH, this HSD affects both NWP CBB Base Die and NIO (Newport IMH die).

The components affected on NWP CBB base die:  CBO

The components affected on NWP NIO is :  AMA



updated NWP Arch spec:

NWP is required to flush all lines from its cache hierarchies when a CLFlush,CLFlushOpt, CLWB is issued by the core, including lines that maybe resident in the PWB at the time of the Clflush,CLFlushOpt, CLWB. To support this requirement, the NWP CBO will always terminate a CLFlush, CLFlushOpt, CLWB flow with an InvXToI. AMA upon receiving the InvXToI will flush the PWB & issue a CleanUnique on the CHI interface (the accel HN does not support receiving a CleanInvalid). The CLWB instruction behaves similar to CLFlush_opt wrt cache invalidations.



When CLFLUSH, CLFLUSHOPT, CLWB instructions are executed:

•CBo issues UXI InvXtoI to AMA unconditionally, incl. when LLC hit
•AMA issues CHI CleanInvalid to GRS unconditionally, incl. when PWB hit
14025926200.title: [NWP] CLFLUSH, CLFLUSHOPT, CLWB instr should flush PWB
14025926200.subject: feature

22021899947.description:
Notes from PnP latency TF:




Original assumptions for buffering were done based on the following diagram, which suggests IO-CA hides write latency from HIOP, and allows HIOP to de-allocate entry on reception of GO_I_WritePull.

 

 

This is not consistent with the IDI spec, nor the SCA implementation. The end result is that HIOP needs to buffer writes for a lot longer than we had calculated.

 

 

So far we had only dealt with growing the OTC CPL Data buffer, but this finding would require ITC data, ITC ordering tracker, CPC Cset and CPC Address CAM entries to grow, on the order of 2-3X (exact details are WIP).

 

Beyond the area impacts of these structures, there does not seem to be line of sight to converging the CPC block at anything meaningfully larger than it is today (DMR tried to grow from 768/832 to ~960 and was not able to converge). COR is working to re-microarchitect the CPC block to allow scalability, but there’s significant risk to deliver this on the NWP schedule / stepping assumptions.

 

The diagram below shows the differences in flow between the current assumption and the correct behavior. The area in yellow corresponds to the transaction lifetime in HIOP, red is TOR lifetime, and blue is WCT lifetime (the latter 2 in SCA).

 

Wrong

Correct

 

 

 

Work is underway to get exact details, but we need to start internalizing the likely impact of

 

I want to clarify that this is orthogonal to the FrO128/WAW/RAR requirements for RO=1 operation that Nimbus communicated. Even if that requirement did not exist, we’d still require additional buffering in HIOP to properly handle MemPushWr. It does add complexity on top of the additional buffering since we need to avoid sending InvItoE to AMA, otherwise that will map to ReadUnique instead of MakeUnique, causing additional overhead on the GRS link.
22021899947.title: [NWP] HIOP buffering impact for MemPushWR RO=1
22021899947.subject: feature

14026756281.description:
This HSD is only tracking HAP & DTI2SFI changes required to support existing IOMMU throughput capability. A separate HSD will need to be filed for any IOMMU changes sourced by IOMMU perf KPI reconciliation with Nimbus.



Initial requirement from the customer was that we needed to support 100MT/s of address translation requests coming from DTI.


The DTI2SFI-->SFI-->HAP-->IOMMU path was plumbed for for ~220MT/s.


We have since learned from the customer that the requirement is 650MT/s once we account for GPU access to CMEM.


Meeting this requirement results in at least a few changes:


- Increase the operating frequency for the DTI2SFI block and the SFI interface from 400MHz to 800MHz. Increases number of stages on SFI bus

- Update HAP buffering to account for both the higher frequency as well as the higher throughput.

- Increased number of SFI stages results in HAP/DTI2SFI having to update their TBN/RBN handling.


We need to understand what the translation performance will be for IOMMU to determine if additional changes (e.g., increase number of PWTRK entries) are required.
14026756281.title: [NWP] HAP & DTISFI changes to support increased ATS KPI requirement from customer
14026756281.subject: feature

  • Notes:

    • VCCCFCMEM is plumbed for PS. But not committed.



In NWP, Ask is to deprecate PS1 for VCCCFCMEM & VCCCFCIO in NIO die for scope redux (considering that focus is on running Fmax)

These domains will be in PS0 

it simplifies multiple items (fivr compensator tuning, efficiency characterization, PS transition validation, per part Vmin search based on PS state etc)

14027021946.description:
Current CMLBUF locations in NWP 0p3 are not optimal and not possible as well based off early simulation data. 

Current locations are, 


cmlbuf50_0 --> parscfmeminf0w
cmlbuf50_1 --> parscfmeminf2w
cmlbuf50_2  --> parmioinf3w
cmlbuf50_3 -->  parmioinf0 
cmlbuf50_4 --> parmioinf3e

These are incorrect, and we have 2 extra. 

New locations will now be. 

cmlbuf50_0 --> parscfmeminf1w
cmlbuf50_1 --> parmiomisc_uio_1 of uio_e stack(non-MI stack)
cmlbuf50_2 --> parmiomisc_uio_1 of uio_e stack(non-MI stack)

cmlbuf50_3 and cmlbuf50_4 will be removed.


I have attached the simulation waveforms and data to determine the locations of the cmlbufs.
14027021946.title: NWP CMLBUF location changes
14027021946.subject: feature

14024580328.description:
General Security Changes (ie Chassis) for NWP
- Access control to/from AMBA CHI device
- SAI changes or conversions


Security Requirements for NWP LPDDR PHY identified:
  • Reads from missing rank shall not adversary to get confidential data or cipher text
  • Writes to missing rank will not result in silent drop
  • Registers in MTE equivalent MUST be only writeable by MMC Firmware (similar to server). 
  • Relevant PHY registers shall be placed in a Policy Group such that an adversary (ie HOST SW or other untrusted agent) cannot change configuration or write capabilities
  • Server SOC is using: SOC_INTEL_FW_SAI to identify MMC

Not meeting these requirements could expose an attack via unpopulated Rank

LPDDR is expected to use S3M for FW load and authentication


NOTE: All the below content was covered in the strawman presentation, to avoid confusion it was decided CAB would be covered in the NWP TDX support feature.
  • GRS IP which is not mentioned in the description below but is in the Strawman slides will be covered under NWP GRS FW update update.
  • LTF discussions will reflect this change. 


  • CAB: Additional requirements will be captured as part of TDX flows

    • Filtering is required to ensure only valid translations are propagated

    • Register based Access Control is required for range registers (PMRRR, SEAMERR)

    • ToDo: Will CAB implement SAI based Policy groups for GRS IP use? Or will GRS IP handle access control. If relying on GRS IP, CAB shall also map the internal IOSF based SAI to AxPROT and AxNSE settings. Need to close on this mapping.

  •  
NWP Device will use two mappings:
 - NSE=0, NS=1: Non-Secure => Non-TEE exclusive memory
 - NSE=1, NS=1: Realm => TEE Exclusive Memory
14024580328.title: [NWP] Security changes to support LPDDR
14024580328.subject: feature

14024559209.description:
This feature is to track changes required to support TDX with LPDDR Memory and NWP customer Accelerator. 

Three different protocols between host and NWP device that TDX arch needs to address

Coherent, ATS, control

Primary data transfer over coherent interface - this must allow transactions to access/use TD private memory from host and device

Unified memory model 

Device access to host memory 

Host access to device memory

Coherent accesses use host physical address

Coherent accesses require ATS translations - ATS translations must allow translations mapping to TD private memory

ATS requests arrive over the high-speed GRS interconnect (ARM DTI internally)

              ATS response must include information that the access is to TD private memory region

Control plane functions over PCIE - MCHECK/ACTM, TDX module and TDs will make use of this to configure information in the device and device function (TDI)

Device enumeration, configuration, SPDM/TDISP

PCIE requires IDE to allow trusted MMIO from TD VM to configure device function assigned to TD
14024559209.title: [NWP] TDX changes to support LPDDR and customer accelerator
14024559209.subject: feature

14027041647.description:
NWP BGRs: NWP BGR placement.xlsx


We need to move two three BGRs from their current locations to new partitions since they are too close to FIVRs, and the partitions they are in do not allow them to be moved far enough away to avoid this interaction.  The two three FIVRs that are problematic and their current/old locations are (from Steven Chang's MAS list):

BGR bgr_memw

bgrgen3hip0

bgr_memw

/imh/subfcscf/scfmem_w/scfmem_w_inf/parscfmeminffivr1w

 

 

BGR bgr_meme

bgrgen3hip0

bgr_meme

/imh/subfcscf/scfmem_w/scfmem_e_inf/parscfmeminffivr1e

 

 
BGR bgr_cabbgrgen3hip0bgr_cab/imh/subfcscf/center/center_mid/parcinf0


 

 

The requirement is that the BGR is ~300um away from the FIVR bump field, which extends about 308um above and below the FIVR's center line:

 

 

The proposal is to move the mem BGRs to the parscfmeminf1w/e partitions, which are the partitions south of where they currently live.  These are long enough that we can get the BGR out from under the shadow of the FIVR bumps

The bgr_cab will move up to the parcinf1 partition.  Here is a summary of the new locations:

bgr_memw: from parscfmeminffivr1w -> parscfmeminf1w

bgr_meme: from parscfmeminffivr1e -> parscfmeminf1e

bgr_cab: from parcinf0 -> parcinf1


26ww08.3 Update: We also need to move the bgr_cab in the center of the die.  I have updated the table above to reflect this.  We originally thought we could keep it in the same partition and simply grow the partition, but it turns out that would cause it to interfere with the IO-mesh routing.
14027041647.title: Need to move three BGRs due to FIVR interactions
14027041647.subject: feature

14027181891.description:
For various reasons, we need to redo the placement of the DTS and remote diodes (RD) for the NIO thermal system.  Some of the current locations either are missing electrical specs or are insufficient to meet the needs the program.  

A discussion about this issue was had in Baseline WG ww09.3 and it was approved to move forward with this pending closure to the ARs.  The PPT for Baseline WG has been attached, but here is a short summary:


The current DTS and diode placement has two problems


1) we don't have diodes near all the places where they are needed.  The FIVRs are apparently some of the hottest parts of the die, and there has been a a request to place a diode near each FIVR

2) the new DTS for this generation has a new max CAP spec that we weren't aware of, which has the result of having the diode routes end up being much shorter than they've been able to be in the past. Todd has gotten us a waiver from the 2pF spec to 3pF, which helps a bit, but still limits the route lengths.


These two things, along with changes like FIVR additions and the UCIe having more diodes than planned, required us to make changes to the thermal system.
The plan is to go from this:



to this:



Details can be found in the attached Baseline WG presentation.  Note that there are still some opens around the C2C diodes.  Here is a list of the opens from Baseline.  Some of these have been closed since then:
14027181891.title: [NWP] NIO: need to redo the DTS and remote diode placement
14027181891.subject: feature

22021884390.description:
Nimbus feedback: WAW is required to be maintained for 64B same address. Fragment ordering of 128B is also required - even 64B line must be older than odd line. 


1) Ensure HIOP is able to support the WAW requirement, RAR ordering is also required

2) Need to characterize perf impact to IO BW KPIs from enforcing Fragment Order of 128B (FO128B)

3) Impact to P2P perf
22021884390.title: [NWP] BW KPI impact from requiring WAW ordering on 64B, 128B (Fragment order) IO streams under relaxed ordering (RO=1)
22021884390.subject: feature

22021968250.description:
The original specsheet from Cadence showed the VDDQX* domains drawing less than 500mA total current for all instances of the DDR PHYs placed in NWP, so the plan was to place a pair of LDOs on the platform.  FIVR or MBVR would be overkill, and waste more power than they actually need to supply.  Since then, Cadence has come back with a number of 3.8A, which would mean the LDOs would be very inefficient, and also require a cooling solution, which is a non-starter.  The direction being investigated now is to add a pair of small VRs to the motherboard to drive these LPDDR6 domains.


With guardband, we'll assume 3.5A on VDDQXD and 1.5A on VDDQXC.  This HSD will track the effort and investigation into placing new MBVRs on the platform.  The proposed solution will have the boot sequence for these driven by the FPGA, and will NOT be connected to SVID.  To cover the step-up requirements from JEDEC, we will implement a switch on the board to pick between 875/1000mV for low voltage, and 900/1025mV for high.  The expectation is that the switch will be set once, and never changed, as NWP will not be supporting DVFS for LPDDR6.

It should be noted that the difference between the voltages is likely within the noise of the VR itself, so it has been requested to check if NWP will utilize the step-up feature.  The CAMM supports it, but we will be driving the CAMM separately.


AR:  Pavan --> determine how to fit these 2 VRs in, and determine if the step-up voltage requirement will be utilized by NWP.
22021968250.title: [NWP] LPDDR6 VDDQXC/VDDQXD/VDDQ supplies
22021968250.subject: feature

22021884362.description:
Nimbus feedback is the device ATC (DTLB) cannot be implicitly trusted. Hence, NWP is required to add support for host-based HPA access checks to enable TDX Connect trust model.


TDX RID Spoofing Mitigation Requirements:

1. Device coherent requests (with HPA) must undergo Stream ID source validation check against an allow list using RID range (base and limit) registers.

2. The RID range registers must be accessible to the TDX module and and use SEAM_OS_W SAI PG.

3. The register location must be communicated to TDX module via MCHECK

4. Note: Nimbus requested that RID spoofing mitigation will be applied even for non CC requests that use IDE.


TDX HAC Management Interface Requirements:

1. HAC page tables shall be aligned with TDX Connect GHPT definition.

2. GHPT page tables shall be managed by the TDX module using private HKIDs to protect the GHPT tables from untrusted software reads/writes.

3. If GHPT table walk fails due to corruption, the HAC go into in-secure mode, blocking all T = 1 traffic allowing TDX module to recover and re-enable HAC.

4. TDX module will update GHPT and IOMMU DMAR tables to wither remove a specific page from a TD, remove a specific device from a TD (RID) or teardown an entire TD (DID).

    HAC must provide TDX module interfaces to invalidate HAC cached HPA permissions. The interface must allow TDX module to confirm there are no cached HAC permissions related to:

    - Specific HPA page (4K, 2M, 1G)

    - All HPAs tagged by a DID

    - All HPAs tagged by a RID (Stream ID)


TDX General Requirements:

1. Although HAC may have multiple instances (i.e. per CHI port), the interfaces to TDX module shall encapsulated as single entity (TDX module cannot be aware of uArch or specific implementation)
22021884362.title: [NWP] Support for host (NWP) based HPA access check
22021884362.subject: feature

14027275854.description:
A 27MHz xtal clock comes from the platform and must be received on the NIO by a GPIO and then distributed to a refclk pin at each C2C HIP with strict jitter specs.  

After the Baseline WG meeting today 26ww10.4, we agreed that adding a GPIO (configured as an RX only) into the C2C misc between the 2 C2C HIPs is the best solution in order to reduce routing complexity and analog risk.


Overall placing in misc reduces analog risk on late evaluation of platform jitter impacting our jitter specs and possibly pushing this to failure requiring redesign at a later time. 

Exact location within the misc can be moved around as we build the partition and learn more from Nimbus about what’s in there.

This new GPIO comes with: GPIO HIP, VNN bumps, VNN clamp for ESD, hardcode inputs to configure as RX only, reset signal?, DFD?



See attached slides for details and simulation data.  Option #1 and Option #2 were presented and option #2 was agreed to be the best path forward. Picture of option #2 below.
14027275854.title: [NWP] GRS 27MHz refclk GPIO receiver location
14027275854.subject: feature

14027372154.description:
Custom RF team recommends converting all RF instances with depths > 128 entries at 2GHz 0.55V usage to avoid silicon failures on 1276.5 process. We need to do this to meet perf at this vmin and before RF team can characterize these arrays and deliver UV collaterals for NWP.


Total of 32 instances identified across D2D (1 instance), HIOP OTC (1 instance) and SCF (30 instances).
14027372154.title: [NWP] NIO: Convert custom RFs with depth > 128 entries for 2GHz 0.55V usage to smaller than 128e
14027372154.subject: feature

Prime Code mailbox will be used to allow BIOS access to some registers in otherwise secured PHY regions.

The ucontroller region is anticipated to be the most impact as BIOS needs to perform configurations, but we want to block access to registers than can lead to FW manipulation.

BIOS should be blocked after configurations and training is complete.


Other registers regions are being evaluated and may be added to the list.
14026944858.title: [NWP] Define LP6 register list for Prime Code Mailbox

14027343287.description:
There has been a request by package to move some of the GPIOs towards the northern side of the die to ease package routing constraints.  This request has been whittled down to just moving the pargiod2d, from the original request to also move the parrclk and HPTP IP.  After the recent changes and growth in C2C, SD is also requesting this change to give us more flexibility to grow C2C towards the east side of the die.  Baseline WG (Jorge and Raj) have approved this change to go to LTF.

Here is a FP picture of the move:



The new location has more space than the old one so there should be sufficient area and bump real estate.


A couple other random details:


- the BGR that is currently in pargpiod2d will have to move to a new partition, probably parseinf0 but I need to confirm that with our flooorplanner.  This HIP is is needed near the current location in the SOC, plus there is already a BGR near the new location.

- the PMAX HIP that is in the same partition will stay in the partition and move to the northwest side.

- CJ has mentioned that several of the chassis IPs needed for the pargpiod2d happen to still live in the infrastructure partition due north of the new location, so that would mean there is a bit less rework here than thought, since these would have had to move to the old location if we don't do this change.  This doesn't mean that there is no work for Chassis here (there is, the BGR change mentioned above for example), but there is less might be expected with a move like this.
14027343287.title: [NWP] NIO: request to move pargpiod2d to the northwest whitespace on the die
14027343287.subject: feature

14026846905.description:
Background: The current NWP uio_a_0 0p3 RTL implementation utilizes a repeater plan originally designed for IMH2. However, the NWP MIO floorplan differs significantly from the IMH2 one, creating a mismatch that requires RTL updates.
Scope of Changes: This update involves two primary modifications:
  1. Removal of existing repeater partitions - Eliminate the IMH2-based repeater structure that is incompatible with the NWP floorplan
  2. Integration of pipeline flip-flops - Insert pipe flops within functional partitions to accommodate the new floorplan requirements
Reference: Detailed NWP repeater plan specifications are attached for implementation guidance.
Impact: This change ensures proper inter-partition connectivity  and timing closure alignment between the RTL implementation and the target NWP floorplan.
14026846905.title: Update NWP 0p3 RTL to Align with NWP MIO repeater plan
14026846905.subject: feature

14027607756.description:
SCF IP team has added 2b of LRU for PWB Cache array and this was not communicated to SoC team.


What this means is 

1) SoC needs to implement 4 instances of 256 x 12 ways * 2b LRU per wau per each PWB instance.

2) Total additional instances = 28 PWB slices * 4 instances * 256 x 24b array


This needs 

1) SoC SD impact

2) Cache IP design change

3) Cache IP val impact

4) Plan to work around this for WW16 drop.
14027607756.title: [NWP] New ask from SCF IP team to support LRU bits in PWB Cache
14027607756.subject: feature

14027547542.description:
This Issue HSD tracks the baseline debug trace capability required for AXI, DTI, and CHI interfaces to the the Nimbus C2C IP with the C2C Stack.  


HAS documentation is in the process of being completed in 26'WW14.  A link will be added here when it is published and ready for review.
14027547542.title: [NWP] NIO: Baseline debug trace capability for AXI, DTI, and CHI interfaces within the C2C stack
14027547542.subject: feature

22022037892.description:
Similar to DMR, NWP needs to update SOC construction so that it can accurately provide time between sockets (socket 0 to socket 1), within a socket (IMH intra-die), and across dies in a socket (IMH to CBB).


1)

inter-socket (S0, S1)

- follow the same as DMR, except NWP only supports 2 sockets. 


NWP is following the same inter-socket time protocol as DMR, which is the IEEE 1588 over S2S SBLink.  Socket 0 IMH.S3M is the time provider.  Socket 1 non-legacy S3M is the time recipient. 



The DMR/OKS cross-socket and cross-die flow uses IEEE 1588 to self-cancel link travel delay.  But if there is residual asymmetry or extra fixed latency in TX/RX paths, that is compensated with TSC_TRANSFER_OFFSET.


NWP has a different platform from DMR/OKS.  So there may be a need to update NWP platform's TSC_TRANSFER_OFFSET register. 


For HAS reference, see: https://docs.intel.com/documents/arch_datacenter/RCF/Global_Time/GNR%20SRF%20DMR/Feature%20HAS/Inter%20Die%20Global%20Time%20Sync.html#introduction-to-egress-and-ingress-alert-signals-for-snapshot-trigger-and-die-external-travel-latency
"soft strap controlled TTRAVEL-LATENCY (that sits in in-pkg CPLD NVRAM), captured in TSC_BOARD_OFFSET, to account for board TTRAVEL-LATENCY. Each SBLink may have a different TTRAVEL-LATENCY."


from https://wiki.ith.intel.com/spaces/PCHLESS/pages/1172413224/CPU+Strap+Support#CPUStrapSupport-OverrideCopyProvisioning, and https://wiki.ith.intel.com/spaces/PCHLESS/pages/1882230950/S3M+2.0+CPU+Straps+HLD,
BIOS/Platform will program TSC_TRANSFER_OFFSET (if needed).

  1. Format of TSC_TRANSFER_OFFSET is below

Bits

Access Type

Default

Description

PowerWell

31

RW

1'h1

VALID_TX_RX_OFFSET_OVERRIDE
When set, TSC_

Primary

30:23

RW

9'h0

Reserved

Primary

22

RW

1’b0

VALID_BOARD_OFFSET_OVERRIDE

When set, Tx & Rx offset have valid override values


21:18

RW

4'h0

TSC_BOARD_OFFSET

This value is to be added to the receiver offset.

Primary

17:9

RW

9'h0

TSC_Rx_OFFSET
This field specifies a value in terms of pm_sync clock cycles to add to the TSC when it is received on the SBLink to account for the delay from the CPU pin to the S3M ART. This is necessary to ensure that CPU-L ART and remote die copies of the TSC are equal at all times.

Primary

8:0

RW

9'h0

TSC_Tx_OFFSET
This field specifies a value in terms of pm_sync clock cycles to add to the TSC when it is being sent out on the SBLink to account for the transmission delay from the ART to the CPU pin. This is necessary to ensure that CPU-PARENT and remote die copies of the TSC are equal at all times.

Primary



2)

Inter die global time sync  (NIO.S3M to each CBB.PUNIT)


NWP will follow the same methodology as DMR.   However, there are NIO (NWP IMH) construction updates/changes.


SOC needs to code "module_flop_repeater_latch" between each D2D stack's SB2UCIe and S3M along ingress/egress alert wires.

S3M FW to account for repeater flops while capturing timestamp over Ingress/egress alert wires from SB2UCIe during D2D 1588. Following fields in the SOC config string need to be updated for inter die global time accuracy -

Soc config string.TSC V2 element

Starting from S3M 3.x --> https://docs.intel.com/documents/security_ip_fw_arch/S3M/FAS/SOC_Configuration/S3M_SOC_Config.html#die-to-die-tsc1588 

// -----------------------------------
typedef struct {
 uint8_t outgoing_delta;
 uint8_t incoming_delta;
 s3m_sb_port_id_t peer_pid;
 uint32_t reserved;
} s3m_die_config_tsc_v2_element;


3)

Intra die global time sync


For NIO intra-die, this is from S3M to PUNIT, and then from PUNIT to various IPs via TSC or HH


Fuse overrides needed for some of the Punit fuses for NWP for intra die global time accuracy -

  • SOC needs repeater flops between Punit and Hammock Harbor IPs (NPK, PCIe RPs) for Sync signal and account of them with PMSRVR_PTPCIOREGS_PTPCBCLK_PMSYNC_CONTROL_TSC_TYPE_B_D_SYNC_OFFSET_FUSE
  • PMSRVR_PTPCIOREGS_PTPCBCLK_PMSYNC_CONTROL_TSC_SEND_OFFSET_FUSE
  • PMSRVR_PTPCIOREGS_PTPCBCLK_PMSYNC_CONTROL_TSC_INIT_OFFSET_FUSE


For CBB intra-die, this TSC download from CBB.PUNIT to all IPs in CBB (such as x86 Cores).

While we do not expect any changes to NWP CBB that will impact Time, TSC, Serial download, Adya is checking with CBB team to confirm.
22022037892.title: NWP SOC implementation for intra die and inter die global time accuracy
22022037892.subject: feature

Milan updated the description of this CCB request:



Details at this link below:

DMR-RS defeature knobs for downbins april 2026.pptx

He's investigating options for using lower binned parts, which can't be sold to Nimbus, as a new product for other customers.  In order to support this, we'll need to ensure the design has a way to fuse disable the C2C GRS IP, as this should only be available in parts supplied to Nimbus. This may already be in place, but want to ensure it's tracked to verify or complete

14026944960.description:
Customer IP is using Register Level Firewalls for access control.


* SOC role: Provide the initiator ID mapping, program the RLF registers, drive the info on the AXI interface


* RLFs are used for checks based on initiator ID granularity as opposed to generic pprot/ns granularity.


* RLFs have a lock bit to prevent further modifications once programmed.
14026944960.title: [NWP] Define Register Level Firewalls - Initiator ID solution
14026944960.subject: feature

14027672807.description:
Based on this HSD:
https://hsdes.intel.com/appstore/article-one/#/14026585116

The LPDDR6 PHY digital logic domains will be driven by an MBVR, providing more flexibility to the NWP SOC and future generations.  This MBVR requires SVID support for telemetry, given that load currents could vary significantly over time.  Per a discussion with Pavan, Vivek, Steven, Don, and Erin, this HSD requests that SVID control be added to NWP PM.  If SVID access is not added, NWP will have to assume a fixed power value and fuse that in for RAPL, which will automatically be pessimistic in nature and may lead to negative performance impacts.
14027672807.title: [NWP] Add SVID control to VCCDDRDIG MBVR domain
14027672807.subject: feature

14027373379.description: For Newport, there is a new (compared to DMR) voltage domain called VCCC2C that controls the analog chip-to-chip interface HIP from Nimbus.  Per Nimbus's requirements, this domain must come up last in the CPU boot sequence, after all other MBVRs and FIVRs.  To do so, we require SVID control so that it can be signaled to ramp up.  In addition, power management and Primecode need telemetry info from this domain, given that it is estimated to draw ~85A.
14027373379.title: [NWP] VCCC2C SVID control
14027373379.subject: feature

fully populated 32 chnl

partially populated 24, 16 chnl

failed-over 28, 16, 8 chnl 


1) all configs except 28 chnl will support mirroring

2) 2/25 NWP Arch WG - Mirror Failover capability not required for NWP
14026977215.title: [NWP CCB] Support for address range (partial) mirroring
14026977215.subject: feature

14027162617.description: Support for 10.7 GT/s (2R), 9.6 GT/s (4R) lpddr6 speed
14027162617.title: [NWP CCB] Support for 10.7 GT/s (2R), 9.6 GT/s (4R) lpddr6 speed
14027162617.subject: feature

14027235624.description:
GNR and DMR connected the VCCFA_EHV MBVR to the SVID controller.  For NWP, space is limited on the platform, and the domain itself is pretty calm/boring -- max current from the latest PnP data is less than 5A.  The domain itself mostly comes up in reset, and drives the LDO inputs for the PLLs and DTS, along with some GPIO drivers.


Given the need for platform space, and the lack of high power draws on this domain, I am recommending that NWP not connect this domain to an SVID controller.
14027235624.title: [NWP CCB] Remove VCCFA_EHV from SVID
14027235624.subject: feature

14027344654.description:
The alignment with the broader team as a result of the CHI latency TF was that each SCA would have the following structure sizes and RTID assignment.



The split between TOR and WCT was based on PnP feedback on the proportion of the latency for write flows between ownership phase and writeback phase.


When the HIOP write buffering issue was sourced, the solution proposed was to move from MemPushWr to WCiLF so we could take advantage of Fast_GO to de-allocate CPC CAM/CTAG.


https://hsdes.intel.com/appstore/article-one/#/22021899947


What we missed at the time was that transactions that use ExtCmp cannot be allocated in the WCT in SCA, and WCiLF requires ExtCmp.


As part of the fragment ordering solution for NWP, the SCA has been requested to support early ExtCmp via a de-feature, where ExtCmp is sent upon ownership being granted, rather than the write-back completing. This change would enable the writeback to be tracked in WCT instead of TOR.


Alternative:


If the risk/cost of the flow change is deemed to high, an alternative option would be to shrink the WCT (to 0 most likely) and grow the TOR instead. The cost of this would be ~100k cells per SCA + 32 TDF RF entries (each ~64B), as well as place additional pressure on timing closure. This alternative proposal would likely result in growth into the center GOT (assuming the design can actually converge).
14027344654.title: [NWP CCB] IOCA to use WCT to track writebacks for WCiLF with early ExtCmp
14027344654.subject: feature

14027456607.description:
Reference Documents:


- Newport C2C AXI/DTI/CHI Protocol Aware Trace Feature HAS

- Newport C2C DFD MAS (Include a subsection for "CHI and DTI Protocol Aware Trace")



Baseline tracing configuration:


Addition covered by this request:



Official request from the customer:

 


Sent: Tuesday, March 17, 2026 4:26 PM
Subject: Re: DTI/CHI Debug Trace Proposal

 

Oleg/Luke,

 

I think the last meeting’s AI was for me to go back and check whether we wanted to make an official request to add SRAM to absorb some streaming traffic so we can see some continuous samples of three channels of 128 bits of choice.

I evaluated with our folks and we think we need this feature. 

Even in our own silicon bringup, we have similar capabilities and we used that for debug. 

 

If I can pinpoint one particular transaction, or one particular type of transactions, I could set trace_tag to filter out noise and the BW requirement might not be high. Even here, it is hard to say whether our <10 GB/s streaming rate is enough or not.

But we have cases where we don’t know what exactly we are looking for, in that case, we want to just look through transactions continuously to figure out what is observed through some window. 

 

We don’t have hard requirement on the size of SRAM per se. That we could check based on your floorpan convenience. 

But one data point we have is: read to GPU round trip (read -> CompData) is 950 ns in loaded system. Maybe we can see at least some response back for some outstanding requests. If the window is too small, we might not see request and response together.

 

Thanks,

Xf

 


Update:


- Ask to also include mask & match block at the output of the muxes used to select a channel for observation.

- Output of mask match is sent as part of the signals for observation (eventually used to qualify FLITV).

- Output of mask/match is also used to manipulate tracetag bit from selected channel
14027456607.title: [NWP CCB] Add SRAM storage to CHI/DTI DVP protocol aware packetizer, Mask&Match and tracetag manipulation capability.
14027456607.subject: feature

14027353461.description:
The cache size of the GPU has changed from 4MB per port aggregation group (PAG) to 8MB.  Two GPUs would then consume 16MB and the HMA SF should be increased from 16MB to 32MB

For 32 ch config 16 SF slices provide 32MB -> 2x coverage

for 24 ch config 12 SF slices provides 24MB -> 1.5x coverage

For 16 ch config 8 SF slices provides 16MB -> 1x coverage - risk of SF thrash


Latest Nimbus feedback on HMA SF sizing -


We are okay with HMA SF sizing of 24MB. Let me know if there is anything else needed to close this on your side.

 

Two top configs are the most important for performance. We are okay here.

24 and 16 have sufficient coverage for the 8MB GPU cache. 

8 channel is non-performant for both GPU and CPU.
14027353461.title: [NWP-CCB] Change GPU cache size to 8MB, HMA SF 24MB
14027353461.subject: feature

14027353323.description: Request from customer that the CHI port interleave should be 256B.  It could be higher, but we agreed on 256B
14027353323.title: [NWP-CCB] Change CHI port interleave to 256B or 512B
14027353323.subject: feature

14027472692.description:
NWP needs to support PWB Clean Evicts to minimize GPU-HA SF capacity evictions which would impact GPU performance.

Core reads will allocate into PWB.

Clean evicts from CBB will transition the PWB state into RC.  RC and RD are priority eviction candidates

RC evictions become CHI clean evicts

There will be no silent evictions
14027472692.title: [NWP CCB]Add PWB Clean Evict support
14027472692.subject: feature

22022032536.description:
This is to track decision to support ITD/Binning on the new fivr domain, VCCC2CDIG.

Referenced from HSD: https://hsdes.intel.com/appstore/article-one/#/article/22021977883


For VCCC2CDIG rail, we need to have the following Primecode fuses to support ITD:

- VCCC2CDIG_ITD_CUTOFF_V

- VCCC2CDIG_ITD_SLOPE

- VCCC2CDIG_ITD_TEMPO_OFFSET

- VCCC2CDIG_ACTIVE_VOLTAGE


Based on https://hsdes.intel.com/appstore/article-one/#/14026585116:  [NWP] VCCFIXDIG tracking, the VCCFIXDIG_E/W will power the MC + HSF.  So we will have ITD/binning support on VCCFIXDIG_E/W rail.
22022032536.title: [NWP] ITD/Binning on Fivr VCCC2CDIG and VCCFIXDIG_E, and VCCFIXDIG_W
22022032536.subject: feature
